title: When can a leaf-wax record support a precipitation-isotope claim?
text: Lake Sugan 38.8667° N, 93.95° E (2,800 m) Qaidam Basin. Sonk11D 41.7939° N, 75.1961° E (3,016 m) Central Tian Shan. extracts, Sugan spans -57 1657 yr BP, Sonk11D spans -45 5989 yr BP.
code: library(leafwax)  example_record_path <- function(filename) {   installed_path <- system.file(     "extdata", "example_records", filename,     package = "leafwax"   )   if (nzchar(installed_path)) {     return(installed_path)   }    source_paths <- file.path(     c("inst", file.path("..", "inst")),     "extdata", "example_records", filename   )   source_path <- source_paths[file.exists(source_paths)][1]   if (!is.na(source_path)) {     return(source_path)   }    stop("Could not locate example record: ", filename, call. = FALSE) }  sugan_path <- example_record_path("LS13WASU_C29_d2H.csv") sugan <- read.csv(sugan_path) sugan$d2h_wax <- sugan$d2H_wax sugan$age     <- sugan$age_yrBP  sonk_path <- example_record_path("LS14LASO_C29_d2H.csv") sonk <- read.csv(sonk_path) sonk$d2h_wax <- sonk$d2H_wax sonk$age     <- sonk$age_yrBP  c(sugan_n        = nrow(sugan),   sugan_range_pm = round(diff(range(sugan$d2h_wax)), 1),   sonk_n         = nrow(sonk),   sonk_range_pm  = round(diff(range(sonk$d2h_wax)), 1)) #>        sugan_n sugan_range_pm         sonk_n  sonk_range_pm  #>           78.0           31.0           98.0          107.3

code: op <- par(mfrow = c(2, 1), mar = c(4.5, 5.4, 2.2, 1), mgp = c(3.4, 0.8, 0))  wax_ylim <- extendrange(c(sugan$d2h_wax, sonk$d2h_wax), f = 0.05)  plot_wax <- function(record, title, boundary, ylim) {   ord <- order(record$age)   plot(     record$age[ord],     record$d2h_wax[ord],     type = "o", pch = 16, col = "black",     xlim = rev(range(record$age)),     ylim = ylim,     xlab = "Age (yr BP)",     ylab = expression(delta^2 * H[wax] ~ "(‰)"),     main = title   )   abline(v = boundary, lty = 2, col = "red") }  plot_wax(sugan, "Lake Sugan (LS13WASU, C29 n-alkane)",          boundary = 800, ylim = wax_ylim) plot_wax(sonk, "Sonk11D (LS14LASO, C29 n-alkane)",          boundary = 5000, ylim = wax_ylim) par(op)

text: local_effective_slope() returns one slope value posterior draw site. value combines global beta_oipc draw spatial slope GP. function returns model draws directly; cap filter . Passing full vector invert_d2H() carries slope uncertainty reconstruction. Passing one number, posterior median, gives point-slope sensitivity run. examples use baseline_sp (spatial intercepts + slope GP, environmental predictors) simplicity. baseline_env_sp companion variant whose detection thresholds appear Figure 5 accompanying manuscript (Bradley 2026); switching model_name change needed use .
code: sugan_lon <- 93.95;   sugan_lat <- 38.8667 sonk_lon  <- 75.1961; sonk_lat  <- 41.7939  slope_sugan <- suppressWarnings(local_effective_slope(   longitude = sugan_lon, latitude = sugan_lat,   model_name = "baseline_sp", n_draws = 100,   verbose = FALSE ))  slope_sonk <- suppressWarnings(local_effective_slope(   longitude = sonk_lon, latitude = sonk_lat,   model_name = "baseline_sp", n_draws = 100,   verbose = FALSE ))  rbind(   sugan = quantile(slope_sugan, c(0.025, 0.5, 0.975)),   sonk  = quantile(slope_sonk,  c(0.025, 0.5, 0.975)) ) #>            2.5%       50%     97.5% #> sugan 0.2453382 0.3878862 0.5338104 #> sonk  0.3050322 0.4423601 0.5633999

text: invert_d2H() uses slope vector previous section. record_id argument tells function rows belong one downcore record. return_full = TRUE keeps posterior draws needed detect_change(). inversion samples analytical uncertainty model residual SD. contrasts within one record, spatial GP intercept cancels sample uses site-level intercept draw.
code: recon_sugan <- suppressWarnings(invert_d2H(   d2H_wax    = sugan$d2h_wax,   d2H_wax_sd = rep(3, nrow(sugan)),   longitude  = rep(sugan_lon, nrow(sugan)),   latitude   = rep(sugan_lat, nrow(sugan)),   model_name = "baseline_sp",   n_posterior_draws = 100,   slope        = slope_sugan,   record_id    = "LS13WASU",   return_full  = TRUE,   verbose      = FALSE ))  recon_sonk <- suppressWarnings(invert_d2H(   d2H_wax    = sonk$d2h_wax,   d2H_wax_sd = rep(3, nrow(sonk)),   longitude  = rep(sonk_lon, nrow(sonk)),   latitude   = rep(sonk_lat, nrow(sonk)),   model_name = "baseline_sp",   n_posterior_draws = 100,   slope        = slope_sonk,   record_id    = "LS14LASO",   return_full  = TRUE,   verbose      = FALSE ))

what: 6. Detection threshold and posterior probability of change
title: When can a leaf-wax record support a precipitation-isotope claim?
text: detect_change() compares interval-mean d2H_precip draws two time intervals. posterior draw, subtracts baseline interval mean test interval mean. returns detection threshold posterior probability interval difference exceeds target magnitudes. example uses sigma_residual = 16, residual scale used package’s spatial-model examples. analysis, use residual SD calibration propagated. estimate_temporal_autocorrelation() estimates lag-1 autocorrelation ordering record age subtracting flat mean. AR(1) estimate simple transparent nearly regular records. Irregular paleo records need sensitivity tests method uneven sampling. Change-point tools bcp Rbeast answer different question: can test whether boundary trend supported, estimate rho_t term detection-threshold formula. Lomb-Scargle option planned v0.3 (method = "lomb_scargle"). Sugan example compares last eight centuries older part record; wax contrast small. Sonk11D example compares 4-5 ka 5-6 ka, extracted C29 n-alkane series much larger contrast. two records differ. Sugan lag-1 autocorrelation 0.2, 95 percent detection threshold 104 per mil, posterior probability 0.04 30 per mil shift. Sonk11D lag-1 autocorrelation 0.72, threshold 54 per mil, posterior probability 1.00 30 per mil shift. example, Sugan contrast small relative calibration noise. Sonk11D contrast large enough 30 per mil quantitative claim pass posterior-probability test.
code: rho_sugan <- estimate_temporal_autocorrelation(   sugan$d2h_wax, sugan$age, method = "ar1" )  dc_sugan <- detect_change(   reconstruction    = recon_sugan,   age               = sugan$age,   baseline_interval = c(-100, 800),   test_intervals    = list(older = c(800, 1700)),   sigma_residual    = 16,   sigma_analytical  = 3,   rho_t             = rho_sugan,   beta_eff          = stats::median(slope_sugan),   confidence        = 0.95,   magnitudes        = c(10, 30, 50) ) #> Warning: leafwax preview posteriors in use (detect_change): 100 draws of #> 'baseline_sp'. Tail probabilities and 95% credible intervals are unstable at #> this sample size; not suitable for inference. Run #> download_model_data("baseline_sp") for the full posterior.  rho_sonk <- estimate_temporal_autocorrelation(   sonk$d2h_wax, sonk$age, method = "ar1" )  dc_sonk <- detect_change(   reconstruction    = recon_sonk,   age               = sonk$age,   baseline_interval = c(4000, 5000),   test_intervals    = list(early_holocene = c(5000, 6000)),   sigma_residual    = 16,   sigma_analytical  = 3,   rho_t             = rho_sonk,   beta_eff          = stats::median(slope_sonk),   confidence        = 0.95,   magnitudes        = c(10, 30, 50) ) #> Warning: leafwax preview posteriors in use (detect_change): 100 draws of #> 'baseline_sp'. Tail probabilities and 95% credible intervals are unstable at #> this sample size; not suitable for inference. Run #> download_model_data("baseline_sp") for the full posterior.  list(   sugan = list(rho_t = round(rho_sugan, 3),                threshold_permil = round(dc_sugan$threshold, 1),                intervals        = dc_sugan$intervals),   sonk = list(rho_t = round(rho_sonk, 3),               threshold_permil = round(dc_sonk$threshold, 1),               intervals        = dc_sonk$intervals) ) #> $sugan #> $sugan$rho_t #> [1] 0.203 #>  #> $sugan$threshold_permil #> [1] 103.8 #>  #> $sugan$intervals #>   interval n_baseline n_test delta_mean delta_median delta_lower delta_upper #> 1    older         41     37  -9.301603    -7.864667   -31.47903    8.930941 #>   p_abs_delta_gt_10 p_abs_delta_gt_30 p_abs_delta_gt_50 #> 1              0.48              0.04              0.01 #>  #>  #> $sonk #> $sonk$rho_t #> [1] 0.716 #>  #> $sonk$threshold_permil #> [1] 54.4 #>  #> $sonk$intervals #>         interval n_baseline n_test delta_mean delta_median delta_lower #> 1 early_holocene         12     15  -137.2436    -137.8394   -200.0781 #>   delta_upper p_abs_delta_gt_10 p_abs_delta_gt_30 p_abs_delta_gt_50 #> 1    -94.7102                 1                 1                 1

title: When can a leaf-wax record support a precipitation-isotope claim?
text: next chunk asks whether record supports Level 4 claim 30 per mil d2H_precip change. evidence strings placeholders show required API structure. replace record-specific literature review. Read verdict$levels top bottom. row reports whether level passed , failed, . run, Sugan fails wax-change step. Sonk11D clears Level 4 interval contrast large example supplies stationarity evidence. stationarity strings placeholders; real Level 4 claim needs record-specific evidence citations.
code: build_claim <- function(beta_eff, rho_t, baseline, test, magnitude_precip) {   list(     level             = 4,     interval_baseline = baseline,     interval_test     = test,     sigma_analytical  = 3,     rho_t             = rho_t,     confidence        = 0.95,     beta_eff          = beta_eff,     magnitude_precip  = magnitude_precip,     corroborating_proxies = list(       regional_proxy = "regional records show coeval shift"     ),     vegetation_stationary = list(       value    = TRUE,       evidence = "n-alkane chain length distributions stable across the boundary"     ),     seasonal_source_stationary = list(       value    = TRUE,       evidence = "regional d18O record shows no seasonality shift"     ),     evapotranspirative_stationary = list(       value    = TRUE,       evidence = "leaf-water proxy stable; no aridity transition"     )   ) }  sugan_record <- data.frame(   d2h_wax     = sugan$d2h_wax,   age         = sugan$age,   d2h_wax_err = rep(3, nrow(sugan)) ) sonk_record <- data.frame(   d2h_wax     = sonk$d2h_wax,   age         = sonk$age,   d2h_wax_err = rep(3, nrow(sonk)) )  verdict_sugan <- suppressWarnings(assess_claim(   record         = sugan_record,   claim          = build_claim(stats::median(slope_sugan),                                 rho_sugan,                                 c(-100, 800), c(800, 1700),                                 magnitude_precip = 30),   reconstruction = recon_sugan ))  verdict_sonk <- suppressWarnings(assess_claim(   record         = sonk_record,   claim          = build_claim(stats::median(slope_sonk),                                 rho_sonk,                                 c(4000, 5000), c(5000, 6000),                                 magnitude_precip = 30),   reconstruction = recon_sonk ))  c(sugan_highest_level  = verdict_sugan$highest_level,   sugan_supported_at_4 = verdict_sugan$asserted_supported,   sonk_highest_level   = verdict_sonk$highest_level,   sonk_supported_at_4  = verdict_sonk$asserted_supported) #>  sugan_highest_level sugan_supported_at_4   sonk_highest_level  #>                    0                    0                    4  #>  sonk_supported_at_4  #>                    1

text: shaded band 90 percent credible interval sample. includes analytical uncertainty, regression-parameter uncertainty, local slope posterior, calibration residual SD. dashed red line marks interval boundary used .
code: op <- par(mfrow = c(2, 1), mar = c(4.5, 5.4, 2.2, 1), mgp = c(3.4, 0.8, 0))  precip_ylim <- extendrange(   c(recon_sugan$summary$d2h_precip_lower,     recon_sugan$summary$d2h_precip_upper,     recon_sonk$summary$d2h_precip_lower,     recon_sonk$summary$d2h_precip_upper),   f = 0.05 )  plot_recon <- function(rec, ages, title, boundary, ylim) {   ord <- order(ages)   plot(     ages[ord],     rec$summary$d2h_precip_median[ord],     type = "n",     xlim = rev(range(ages)),     ylim = ylim,     xlab = "Age (yr BP)",     ylab = expression(delta^2 * H[precipitation] ~ "(‰)"),     main = title   )   polygon(     c(ages[ord], rev(ages[ord])),     c(rec$summary$d2h_precip_lower[ord],       rev(rec$summary$d2h_precip_upper[ord])),     border = NA, col = adjustcolor("steelblue", alpha.f = 0.3)   )   lines(ages[ord], rec$summary$d2h_precip_median[ord],         type = "o", pch = 16, col = "black")   abline(v = boundary, lty = 2, col = "red") }  plot_recon(recon_sugan, sugan$age,            "Lake Sugan (LS13WASU): small interval contrast",            boundary = 800, ylim = precip_ylim) plot_recon(recon_sonk, sonk$age,            "Sonk11D (LS14LASO): large 4-6 ka contrast",            boundary = 5000, ylim = precip_ylim) par(op)

title: When can a leaf-wax record support a precipitation-isotope claim?
text: record’s ability support d2H_precip claim depends three quantities: interval-mean d2H_wax contrast relative sigma_residual, local effective slope, lag-1 temporal autocorrelation. detect_change() combines threshold posterior probability. assess_claim() checks result four claim levels. Small contrasts can fail first step. Large contrasts can support directional quantitative claims, Level 4 claim still requires independent evidence stationarity.

title: When can a leaf-wax record support a precipitation-isotope claim?
text: vignette uses 100 posterior draws runs quickly. final reconstructions, use full posterior omitting n_draws n_posterior_draws. estimate_temporal_autocorrelation() uses flat-mean lag-1 estimator works best nearly regular spacing. irregular paleo records, use sensitivity analyses uneven-sampling autocorrelation method rho_t. Use bcp Rbeast complementary change-point trend checks, direct rho_t estimators.

Inversion with the defended slope recon <- invert_d2H(   d2H_wax    = record$d2h_wax,   d2H_wax_sd = record$d2h_wax_err,   longitude  = rep(-90, nrow(record)),   latitude   = rep( 38, nrow(record)),   model_name = "baseline_sp",   slope      = slope,   record_id  = "your_record_id",   return_full = TRUE )  # 3. what: Assess a paleoclimate claim against the leaf-wax taxonomy — assess_claim
title: Assess a paleoclimate claim against the leaf-wax taxonomy — assess_claim
text: Walks four-level taxonomy manuscript Section 4.5.6 reports highest level claim survives . taxonomy : Level 1: leaf-wax delta-2-H change occurred two intervals. Defensible change exceeds analytical uncertainty. Level 2: wax change consistent directional hydroclimate change. Requires corroborating evidence (multi- proxy concordance, sedimentological context, biomarker evidence vegetation stability) supplied via corroborating_proxies. Level 3: wax change implies quantitative delta-2-H_precip magnitude. Requires defended local effective slope explicit uncertainty propagation inversion. reconstruction NULL function calls invert_d2H() . Level 4: magnitude uniquely attributable precipitation isotope change rather vegetation, source-water seasonality, evapotranspirative enrichment. Requires independent stationarity evidence non- precipitation control interval.

title: Assess a paleoclimate claim against the leaf-wax taxonomy — assess_claim
text: record Data frame (list) least d2h_wax age columns equal length. d2h_wax_err optional; defaults claim$sigma_analytical per row. claim Named list specifying claim. Required fields: level (integer 1-4, level user asserting), interval_baseline (length-2 numeric c(min, max) age window), interval_test (length-2 numeric age window). Optional fields, used higher levels: sigma_analytical (default 3), rho_t (default 0; estimate_temporal_autocorrelation()), beta_eff (numeric scalar; required Level 3+), confidence (default 0.95), magnitude_precip (numeric, precip-space magnitude user asserts; required Level 3+), corroborating_proxies (list, used Level 2; test non-empty + named), vegetation_stationary, seasonal_source_stationary, evapotranspirative_stationary (list value (TRUE) non-empty evidence string; required Level 4). reconstruction Optional output invert_d2H(..., return_full = TRUE) record. NULL claim's level 3 4, function runs inversion . longitude, latitude Site coordinates, used reconstruction NULL. model_name Model use running inversion (default "baseline_sp"). ... Additional args forwarded invert_d2H() (e.g., elevation, c4_fraction, pft_*, n_posterior_draws).

title: Assess a paleoclimate claim against the leaf-wax taxonomy — assess_claim
text: list elements: highest_level - integer 0:4. 0 means even Level 1 clear. levels - data frame, one row per level, columns level, passed (logical), summary (one-line reason). details - per-level lists computed quantities (e.g., delta_wax, threshold, p_exceed, missing fields). claim - (validated) claim object.

Models include: baseline: Basic OIPC model without spatial effects baseline_sp: Basic model spatial Gaussian process baseline_env: Includes precipitation-amount effects baseline_env_sp: Precipitation-amount effects spatial GP baseline_veg: Includes vegetation interaction effects baseline_veg_sp: Vegetation interactions spatial GP c4_only_sp: C4 vegetation effects (spatial) elevation_only_sp: Historical elevation-context variant (spatial) elevation_c4_sp: Historical elevation-context + C4 variant elevation_c4_interact_sp: Historical elevation/C4-interaction name; C4 effect full: Precipitation amount + vegetation interactions without spatial component full_sp: Precipitation amount + vegetation interactions spatial component full_interact: Precipitation amount + vegetation interactions full_interact_sp: Full interaction model spatial GP

title: Within-record d2H_precip change detection — detect_change
code: detect_change(   reconstruction,   age,   baseline_interval,   test_intervals = NULL,   sigma_residual,   sigma_analytical = 3,   rho_t = NULL,   beta_eff,   confidence = 0.95,   magnitudes = NULL )

title: Within-record d2H_precip change detection — detect_change
text: reconstruction Output invert_d2H(..., return_full = TRUE) downcore series. Must contain posterior_draws matrix shape n_iter x n_samples. age Numeric vector, length n_samples, sample ages matching reconstruction columns. baseline_interval Length-2 numeric c(min, max) defining baseline window age units. test_intervals Either length-2 numeric vector single test window, named list length-2 numerics multiple windows. NULL skips per-interval probability table returns threshold. sigma_residual Numeric, required, model's posterior residual SD leaf-wax per-mil scale (sigma, approximately 16 per mil spatial models; see Section 4.5.3). sigma_analytical Numeric, analytical uncertainty d2H_wax measurements per mil (default 3). rho_t Numeric, lag-1 temporal autocorrelation. Use estimate_temporal_autocorrelation() compute. Defaults 0 (independent samples) message. beta_eff Numeric, local effective slope. Use local_effective_slope() point estimate (e.g., median). confidence Numeric (0, 1), confidence level detection threshold. Default 0.95. magnitudes Optional numeric vector magnitudes (per mil) evaluate posterior Pr(|delta| > magnitude) .

title: Within-record d2H_precip change detection — detect_change
text: list elements: threshold - detection threshold d2H_precip requested confidence level. formula - components used: z, rho_t, sigma_residual, sigma_analytical, beta_eff. intervals - data frame one row per test interval reporting posterior median CI delta (magnitudes supplied) posterior probability exceeding magnitude.

title: Within-record d2H_precip change detection — detect_change
text: $$\mathrm{threshold}_{precip} =       \frac{z_{\alpha/2}\, \sqrt{2(1 - \rho_t)}\,             \sqrt{\sigma_{residual}^2 + \sigma_{analytical}^2}}            {\beta_{\mathrm{eff}}}$$ threshold smallest difference d2H_precip two independent samples can distinguished within-record noise chosen confidence level. spatial GP intercept contributes constant every sample record cancels contrast; sigma_residual spatial calibration applies (manuscript Section 4.5.3).

text: longitude Numeric, single longitude decimal degrees. latitude Numeric, single latitude decimal degrees. model_name Character, v10 model name (see available_models()). Must spatial model (*_sp) site-specific slope differ global mean; non-spatial models return global posterior unchanged. override Optional numeric. NULL (default) uses model slope. single value broadcasts across draws. vector length n_draws used per draw. n_draws Integer, optional number posterior draws use (NULL uses ). Forwarded load_posteriors(). verbose Logical, whether print progress messages.

title: Local effective slope at a paleo-reconstruction site — local_effective_slope
text: Numeric vector length n_draws, per-draw effective slope site (override, ).

title: Local effective slope at a paleo-reconstruction site — local_effective_slope
text: Two modes: Default: returns model's per-draw slope site. Override (single value per-draw vector) replaces model slope defended local value (e.g., independent evidence source-water seasonality, leaf-water enrichment, vegetation). Pass returned vector invert_d2H(..., slope = ...) propagate inversion. Mechanistic reference values (e.g. simple two-pool stationarity bound alpha = 1 + epsilon_app/1000 ~ 0.88 epsilon_app ~= -120 permil; Sessions 2005) documented interpretation never applied returned draws. frequency draws chosen reference computable directly returned vector (mean(slope > 0.88)) carries scientific information often calibration implicates non-stationarity site.

title: leafwax 0.2.2
text: invert_d2H() invert_d2h(): reported credible interval include model’s posterior residual SD sigma, reported prediction_interval_width wrong quantity single-site reconstruction. typical site gave intervals roughly order magnitude narrower manuscript’s per-site uncertainties. wax-error draw now follows manuscript supplement Section S4.1 Eq. 7: analytical^2 + sigma_residual^2 combined quadrature, parameter spatial uncertainty carried per-iteration posterior draws. applies uniformly absolute single-point reconstruction within-record contrasts; spatial GP intercept’s contribution cancels contrast computed returned posterior_draws (manuscript Section 4.5.3). detect_change(): threshold-formula argument renamed sigma_within sigma_residual match manuscript’s framework. Pass model’s posterior sigma (approximately 16 per mil spatial models). function longer requires reconstruction built special mode. assess_claim(): dropped claim$sigma_within field. L1 threshold uses analytical uncertainty alone (manuscript Section 4.5.3); L3 still uses inversion’s posterior_draws longer requires separate within-record SD. Removed estimate_sigma_within(). Within-record uncertainty framework comes calibration’s sigma_residual combined spatial GP intercept’s cancellation contrast; separate record-specific SD estimate. invert_d2H_ensemble(): rewritten fix multiple bugs default-args path. previous default models = argument used v0.1 names v10 registry; replaced c("full_sp", "full_interact_sp", "elevation_c4_interact_sp"). Validation accessed available_models()$model, available_models() returns character vector, subset always NULL validation silently skipped; now reads vector directly. inner invert_d2H() call pass return_full = TRUE, pooling step downstream read $posterior_draws summary data frame got NULL; now forces return_full = TRUE. pooling step flattened per-model posterior_draws matrix across draw site axes, collapsing multi-site input one scalar mean shared across sites; now pools per-site, per-draw across models. pool also previously concatenated per-model draws sampled, gave models draws (e.g. 1000-draw heavy tier vs. 100-draw preview tier) proportionally larger share “equal” pool; now resamples model uniform n_target / k draws first concatenates. Return shape changed (see Breaking changes ). compare_models(): default models = argument referenced v0.1 names; replaced c("baseline", "baseline_sp", "full_sp"). Several latent failures also blocked default invocation. verbose partial-match conflict forwarding ... predict_d2h_precip() producing formal argument "verbose" matched multiple actual arguments; switched .call() filtered extra-args list. Single-row input made per-model means length-N vector NULL dim(), tripping row-wise apply(); coerced 1xN matrix case. per-model column rename return_all = TRUE applied unconditionally broke ensemble-summary path; now applied return_all = TRUE path. user typo ... (e.g. verb = FALSE) used cause every per-model .call() fail “unused argument”, per-model tryCatch swallow failure warning, function abort misleading “models failed”; compare_models() now validates ... predict_d2h_precip()’s formals front reports unknown argument names clear error model loop runs. models_used field returned ensemble summary used report originally requested set rather models actually contributed; now reports names(model_results) partial-failure runs silently misreported. predict_d2h_precip() invert_d2H(): c4_fraction * 100 conversion unconditional, NULL input became numeric(0) tripped spurious capability-mismatch warning inside core inversion. wrappers now keep NULL NULL. also now reject vector-length mismatches c4_fraction d2H_wax (: silent R recycling generic warning) validate user inputs lie [0, 1]. --range error names offending maximum value tells caller divide 100 input percent scale. get_data_manifest(): previously returned list(files = list()) download failure, downstream verify_data_integrity() read “checksums available, allow file.” Now returns NULL warning(); verify_data_integrity() treats NULL “verification skipped” warns explicitly. check_data_cache(), list_cached_models(), get_cache_files(): download_model_data() writes single posterior file per model posteriors/<model>_posterior.rds, three helpers still looking v0.1 layout (metadata/<model>_metadata.rds, posteriors/<model>_2000draws.rds, posteriors_full/<model>_complete.rds). successful download reported model absent. three helpers now read canonical v0.2 layout. data_type argument check_data_cache() retained API compatibility now -op. data_urls.json: base_url_latest previously pointed main branch bradleylab/leafwax-data manifest_url pinned v1.0.1. Aligned v1.0.1 future non--op verify_data_integrity() trip drift latest-branch files v1.0.1 manifest. clear_download_cache() longer creates cache directory checking exists. Replaced default get_cache_dir() call get_cache_dir(create = FALSE).

title: leafwax 0.2.2
text: fixes resolve issues surfaced pre-CRAN review pass. invert_d2H_ensemble() aborted “formal argument ‘return_full’ matched multiple actual arguments” whenever caller passed return_full (model_name) .... wrapper now strips names ... per-model loop. function also now warns models contribute unequal draw counts pools model median count rather first model’s count. process_sequential() referenced processing_time outside (progress) {...} block assigned, throwing “object ‘processing_time’ found” whenever batch_predict(..., progress = FALSE) called. batch_predict() aborted “numbers columns arguments match” one chunk errored smaller fallback shape another succeeded full schema. combine step now uses column-tolerant helper (.rbind_chunks) pads missing columns NA. detect_change() aborted rbind mismatch empty test_interval paired magnitudes. empty- interval branch now appends NA magnitude columns column set matches populated rows. “elevation” code path invert_d2h() unreachable every v10 model. None 14 fitted posteriors carry beta_elev columns, model$elevation never populated function unconditionally hit “knots found” warning silently dropped user-supplied elevation. Removed dead spline branch; has_elevation now derived actual posterior columns FALSE every shipped v10 model. “env” variants instead carry beta_precip term, exposed via metadata$has_precip. validate_inputs(): validated PFT vectors dropped returned list model used v10 has_vegetation flag (rather legacy has_pft). output now honours names. download_with_progress(): error handler called close() con_in / con_out unconditionally, raising secondary error inside handler either yet opened. handler now closes connections actually opened.

title: leafwax 0.2.2
text: Removed unreachable lookup-table subsystem (R/lookup_tables.R, R/lookup_integration.R, predict_spatial_mpp() deprecated stub, use_lookup argument predict_d2h_precip()). path never wired shipping data archive; spatial predictions now always go predict_spatial_dual_gp() live posterior. Removed clear_data_cache(). Use clear_download_cache() instead; two helpers near-duplicates behavioural difference. Removed dead exports: batch_invert_d2h(), monitor_memory(), verify_data_integrity(), setup_leafwax_data(), select_best_model(), get_model_recommendations(). None callers package several relied broken legacy paths. Removed misleading text-progress bar predict_d2h_precip(): jumped 0% 100% single step regardless work done, inversion runs one pass per-iteration callback. progress argument retained now -op layer; chunked progress reporting still driven batch_predict().

title: leafwax 0.2.2
text: invert_d2H() reported intervals now wider (posterior predictive includes residual sigma). point estimate (d2h_precip_mean, d2h_precip_median) unchanged; d2h_precip_sd, d2h_precip_lower, d2h_precip_upper, prediction_interval_width wider. invert_d2H() invert_d2h(): removed sigma_within sigma_within_sd arguments. function applies calibration’s sigma_residual directly. detect_change(): renamed argument sigma_within sigma_residual. assess_claim(): removed required claim$sigma_within field. estimate_sigma_within() removed. invert_d2H_ensemble() return shape changed. posterior_draws now n_draws x n_sites matrix (previously: flat vector length n_draws single-site, silently corrupted multi-site). ensemble_summary now data frame one row per site (previously: list scalars, correct single-site input). list pooled models now exposed top level models_used; previous ensemble_summary$models_used gone. Single-site code read e$ensemble_summary$mean continues work — data frame one row, $mean returns single value. Multi-site code breaking definition: callers reading wrong numbers fix. exported data objects model_metadata, mini_posteriors, mini_lookup_table removed. held v0.1 model names used v10 code path; metadata now exposed via get_all_model_metadata() posteriors via load_posteriors() (lazy-load resolver). Users still calling data(model_metadata) see “data set found” switch get_all_model_metadata(). legacy v0.1 helpers load_model_posteriors(), check_model_data(), use_example_data(), get_model_size_estimate() removed. New code call load_posteriors(), check_data_cache(), lazy-load download path. synthetic-data fallback inside use_example_data() also gone; missing posteriors now produce explicit error instead fabricated draws. internal .download_model_data_v0_1() private helper get_download_files() removed (exported download_model_data() R/download_data.R unchanged). validate_inputs()’s default model_name changed "b0b1" "baseline". previous default v0.1 name v10 registry, calls relied default errored get_all_model_metadata() lookup; new default closest v10 equivalent. compare_models()’s NULL-fallback model set changed v0.1 names ("b0b1", "b0b1_elev", "b0b1_sp") v10 ("baseline", "baseline_sp", "full_sp"). rationale: old default unreachable. lookup-table API removed (create_lookup_table(), use_lookup_if_available(), predict_spatial_mpp(), validate_lookup_table(), get_spatial_params(), cache_all_lookup_tables(), benchmark_lookup(), generate_global_grid(), plus print.leafwax_lookup_table method). path never wired published data archive; v0.2 caller used . predict_d2h_precip(): removed use_lookup argument (-op since lookup-table subsystem gone). clear_data_cache() removed; use clear_download_cache(). batch_invert_d2h(), monitor_memory(), verify_data_integrity(), setup_leafwax_data(), select_best_model(), get_model_recommendations() removed.
